behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT model’s diarization/labeling quality, not just local wiring. A mock STT response would hide mislabeling failures (MOCK-HIDE), and correctness depends on real model output variability (LLM-DEP-style AI/ML dependency). System was considered but rejected because the behavior text focuses on label correctness, not transport/OS/network failure. Workflow was considered and rejected: behavior text describes one model decision point (STT speaker labeling), not multiple agentic stages that must all be validated in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real STT provider, then verify speaker-labeled transcript segments map correctly to attendee attributions used by summarization input/output checks.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is deterministic parsing/structuring logic inside a local boundary (MOCK-MISS says this is one-function/object style unless cross-module failure is explicit). No required real OS/network/subprocess (not System), no dependence on model judgment quality (not Agentic), and no multi-agentic chain (not Workflow). Integration was considered and rejected because the behavior does not explicitly require cross-module handoff to fail.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function crafted transcript lines with explicit assignments and assert exact structured output fields (`assignee`, `task_text`) and edge-case parsing (punctuation, duplicate names, multiline tasks).

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real summarization LLM includes/omits for each assignee; deterministic stubs can always pass and hide real omission failures (deterministic-assertion trap, MOCK-HIDE). Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is about output content quality, not only orchestration. Workflow was considered and rejected because this behavior can be validated at a single model decision boundary (summary generation from transcript), without requiring multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each attendee with an assignment appears with at least one action item in structured output across repeated runs/tolerance criteria.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior wording targets routing logic after decisions are already identified; that is a local transformation concern. No real infra required (not System), no dependence on model decision quality for this specific behavior text (not Agentic), no multi-step chain (not Workflow). Integration was considered and rejected because the text does not require cross-module failure evidence beyond a local mapper/router.
behaviors.test_description: Call decision-routing function with fixtures containing pre-identified decision items and narrative text, then assert decisions are placed only in `decisions` schema fields and excluded from generic narrative containers.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is content-selection/extraction quality of the real LLM output; mocks hide whether the model actually promotes decisions into the right section (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP). Integration was considered and rejected because behavior text checks semantic output quality, not just field plumbing. Workflow was considered and rejected because the behavior itself does not require validating multiple independent model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript inputs containing explicit decisions through the live summarization model and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` fields and absent from narrative-only placement criteria.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-stage processing and merge across segments; this is a chained pipeline with repeated model-dependent steps (MULTI-STEP). Agentic alone is insufficient because it would not validate handoff/order/merge correctness across multiple segment runs. Integration was rejected by INTEGRATION-BRAKE: correctness depends on AI output coherence across steps and orchestration state propagation, which exceeds simple module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, execute per-segment transcription/summarization sequentially, merge outputs, and verify final summary coherence plus completeness across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end orchestration over multiple stages, including real STT and LLM calls, with sequencing and state handoff across the full pipeline (MULTI-STEP). Lower levels miss real cross-stage failures; Integration fails INTEGRATION-BRAKE due to real model dependence and stage chaining, and System alone is too narrow because success criterion spans agentic content stages plus orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real meeting recording through production upload-to-delivery pipeline and assert unattended completion, valid structured schema, and successful stage transitions with no manual recovery.
